purpose: Reusable scaffold for supervised and deep supervised learning tasks such as demand forecasting, tabular regression, quantile regression, classification, ranking, and surrogate prediction inside OR workflows.
intended_use: Copy this folder into a deployment workspace when a confirmed method needs supervised learning.
Select an algorithm family from algorithms/ before implementing task-specific code.
Fill data, feature, model, loss, runner, evaluation, and run-record contracts for the concrete task.
Training must monitor validation or downstream metrics, save the best model, and use that best model for final evaluation by default.
Training deployments must define a credible fallback model or rule before pilot runs and evaluate it under the same protocol if training is unstable, underperforms, or exhausts the redesign budget.
Keep training logs and artifacts compatible with run_records/ templates.
non_goals: This asset is not a complete runnable package.
This asset does not prescribe one library or one model class.
This asset does not contain task data, trained models, or domain-specific feature logic.

This asset must not be used to generate toy or minimal examples that omit leakage-safe splits, strong baselines, validation metrics, best-model checkpointing, fallback-model planning, progress output, and downstream decision evaluation when relevant.

top_level_paths.algorithms: Algorithm catalogs and high-level estimator contract.
top_level_paths.data: Data loading, schema, split, and leakage-control notes.
top_level_paths.features: Feature pipeline contract and feature governance notes.
top_level_paths.losses: Loss and scoring contract plus common objective catalog.
top_level_paths.models: Model family catalog and prediction model contract.
top_level_paths.scripts: Foreground shell templates for short runs, user-facing run scripts, and compact result collection.
